review: recently, the whisper function of the app sporadically breaks. when this happens, I can't enter, submit, or speak a new prompt in any way. it's fully frozen for that conversation until I stop and relaunch the app. I really like speaking my prompts, but it's very disheartening to submit a long-winded recording and immediately realize it's been thrown into the garbage. I'm hesitant to trust the app with my input.

I like it a lot, great for helping me with writing my stories, but it's kinda limited if you don't have the money to pay for the Supreme version, I'd like if it was just free and had less restrictions, but also currently ive been trying to make stories and it is telling me that my request is not allowed and to try again later, waited a few hours, still doing the same thing, until fixed it will remain 3 stars sorry.

review: I waited an entire day for the AI to respond, and it still just keeps buffering. No matter what i do. I even updated it last night, it STILL didn't work. I deleted it and redownload it twice, and opened and closed it. IT ISN'T WORKING. The only reason I'm giving it two stars instead of 1 is because it DID work at one point. Please get me some help.

I don't normally leave reviews on apps but I have to for this one. As a nursing student who learns a lot from practice tests, ChatGPT has been a great resource at making practice tests tailored to my textbook and my syllabus! I can also ask if specific questions and have it break down confusing concepts in ways that are easy for me to grasp. It's also helped me a lot personally such as creating a workout routine, skincare routine, meal plan, and so much more!! ChatGPT is a literal LIFESAVER.

My experience with chatgpt has always been absolutely amazing, but from the last few days the mic option is not working which makes it very hard to work with as it's much faster to say the things instead of typing it all out. It would be highly appreciated if this is fixed. Otherwise 5 stars all the way.

The app is amazing at first but I'm a simple man and just want to use one chat log and it seems to overfill easy and the system gets forgetful when you ask for a specific command like for example i would want recipes broken down in certain. Way and the ai would do it my way for the first recipe then later when I ask about another recipe it brakes it down completely different than the original format that I asked it to remember and I would have to double check info for accuracy n it's the paid V

Love it for some things, but it's glitches up right now and forgets elements of conversations and repeats errors corrected during the chat. Not just prior chats, but in the very same session. It's like talking to a person with a learning disability (eg, rainman) and having to reteach it the outcome of the prior conversation. It'd like the session memory cache is limited to next to nil. Better than Google search, usually, but she's in her second timeout due to lack of effort (Google spot on).

ChatGPT is a highly versatile tool. It's easy to get started with, but after many months of using it daily, I'm still discovering more ways to make it useful. It helps me code more efficiently by finding my errors and explaining concepts I may have misunderstood. It helps to find objective stances on politics and interpersonal problems. It helps me with D&D character builds. The list goes on. It has areas of notable weakness, but its usefulness is limited mainly by my own creativity.

The application is great; however, I gave it three stars because it has become bad at solving the problems I present to it. It doesn't provide clear answers for chemistry problems but rather gives incorrect outputs with programming errors and incomprehensible symbols, as if there is a glitch in the app. I hope this issue gets resolved quickly because I have exams and need this application. I even updated it, but I still see the same problem.

A great tool to use especially for writing. It really carries me when I'm writing an essay or working on one of my stories. I can't comment on the image generation or audio because I don't use those features. I've had no real issues, other than one. The response limit is hit or miss, some days it gives me a lot, others not so much. But it's okay because you can still chat after the limit, it'll just assign a different bot for a couple hours

this is my favorite ai. I use it probably 1-15 times per day. I find it super helpful and easy to use and comforting to talk to. my favorite feature is cross-chat memory. wish you could adjust the "read aloud" option speed because it can be kind of slow, especially for long bodies of text. wish there was a widget on my home screen like Google to type into or start voice convo automatically. wish voice convo was streamlined to launch; only works reliably if I press to hold to speak at a time.

Fantastic app! Very human-like in responses, much like AI in the movie Her. I wish the sky voice was brought back. The voice resembled Scarlett Johansson's. Regardless, the app is still immersive, entertaining and knowledgeable. Little impact on battery and memory. It would be nice if there was no conversation cap/limit in the free version. The subscription of $20 a month seems a little exorbitant. However, if you can afford it... I would go for it. Highly recommended

Wonderful! I pretty much completely stopped using Google and other services in place of ChatGPT. I'm excited to see more features such as useful notifications (calender alerts, timers, alarms, etc...) to provide a more tangible and practical means of using it, instead of just a simple search engine. In other words, I want more ways to use GPT instead of just a search engine, or picture generator, or etc.... I'm really excited to experience the future of ChatGPT and how it can benefit my life

Emoji button is gone from the text status option. Why is that this app's update always makes it worse than before. I get that you did make an improvement (unnoticeable as always), but the things that has been removed or changed not to the users liking always sticks out the most. And one more thing. The web version should at least make text status. People who works 24/7 on their PC might need to use that anyway. And now somehow my contact isn't linked to this app

review: This app is sus af. Cheap stuff and painless return policy is a plus, but that's just the necessary tradeoff for hit or miss quality items and unreliable reviews/listing information. Otherwise, way fewer people would ever come back. But that's not all. The extra gimmicks and popups are a massive waste of time at best and manipulative marketing at worst. (They offer "free" items that cost you way more than they're worth just in time spent on the app, not even counting the extra purchases needed.)

They did their usual bait and switch. Offer a discount/promo, and then when time to check out, those items disappear after payment made. Spoke with customer service and was told I might have not met requirements. I spent more than was required and still had time left. Their response was to send me links to cancel my order..which would have been great, IF they worked. So I ended up buying stuff for a promo that I never got. Then they text saying I can buy those items in a new order.

Very bad. Never get the deals they offer. You get 100% free item but have to buy $35 worth of stuff. Supposed to get part of previous order cash back but have to spend min. of $35 and when I did that I never got any money. Offer you certain number items free but don't. The stuff you get are smaller than the pictures show and things look cheap and break easy. I do like the clothes except you have to get 2 sizes bigger than you normally wear

The app is okay, but you can spend hundreds of dollars trying to get the "free" stuff from the games and still get nothing. You move up quickly until you get to 99.9 percent, then it takes thousands of points, moves, gallons of water, whatever to get the last .1 percent. over 10,000 gallons of water for the last .1 percent and it only gave me .05 percent, then it lowered the points I get for spending water for the last half percent. Don't bother with the games. It's a waste of time and money.

Ordered one item because of the promotions which sounded very good. I did get the item, but then unexpectedly the other 4 items that were suppose to have arrived at the same time somehow got lost in transit....yeah, because they were the free items that for some reason got shipped separately. I have a PO Box mainly for my packages, so i don't buy this excuse. This app is a scam and now I confirmed it and since it's a Chinese app, then yeah, it's a scam.

I have had extensive experience. I mostly bought items for a children's charity. I also bought household products and gifts for family. Rating: For the variety of products, 100 stars. For price and quality across the board, an average of 3.5 stars. For efficiency of ordering, shipping and returns, 4 stars. For marketing, 3 stars (too much) For telephone customer service, 4 stars (I could always reach a person) For coupon and game promotions, 1 star (confusing, time-consuming, disappointing)

Contrary to belief, the products have great texture style and something different from local stores. They give more than enough time to decide if you want to keep it, and the return is simple. Except for the first time. Refunds are never questions and you have many choices from which to pay. Great sales.im really happy I found this app. For all you doubting Thomas's, test it out. More importantly, the sizing is closer to correct than US stores. Usually it's a hemming problem.

Most of the products I have ordered are spot on perfect! Always make sure you read the dimensions/size information and do not go by just a picture! also, it is important to read the description anyway so you know exactly what you are getting. Temu is a good source of affordable items. only thing that was horrible was a set of pillows, which had minimal "stuffing". Not their fault, but make sure to read everything in the ad and their "promotions", you don't get those coupons in one order.

Free items are NOT FREE! When offered discounts, there's a lot of hoops to jump through. They have a Farmland game. It claims if u do everything they ask, I would get a couple free items. I've been working for 2 months, referred people who made orders, I've made multiple orders & still no free items! It asks u to water a garden. At 1st, it adds up quickly. When u get to 99% it gives you tiny tiny amounts to keep u going, but you never reach 100%. Their items are ok, though. U get what u pay 4!

Got a deal where I could pick out a bunch of items for free but had to still make a purchase over $50 to get them. The main issue is that the last item became invalid so now I have to redeem something else and none of the other items available come close to what was offered before. You would think the items would've been put aside but I suppose not. Not gonna be returning to this app again going forward.

So far ordered a cat bed ended up being larger then I expected and our mini-aussies love it. App has a lot of good choices too many to choose from. Lots to sort through! I really dislike that they state you get "100% discount or credit back" I have ordered quite a few things and have yet to receive any of the free items or money back without spending more money. Yes they have some items at good prices but make sure you search as many items have multiple listing's!

I could never figure out how to buy certain items workout purchasing 3 item groups. also, every time I open it, it asks you to spin a wheel like 6 times. I tried it about year ago. it frustrated me and I never checked out. 2ND ATTEMPT. I could not find a way to add the new item I wanted by itself without spinning some wheel or adding some bundle. I'M DONE. un-installed. I found the item I wanted for $6 more on Amazon. I'd rather pay more than deal with TEMU. Lame.

The gameplay is enjoyable, though the experience can be heavily skewed negatively if you are missing a category of character. The story is enjoyable but does have grammatical errors and sometimes missing letters or words entirely on occasion. Progression came to a swift halt around midway through chapter 5. My current biggest gripe is needing to uninstall and reinstall every time there's a seemingly substantial update.

A lot of past content is now being saved on its own page, but I can imagine new players getting overwhelmed by all of the things in the menu. There's also issue of current events being back-to-back, resulting in burnout for some players.

review: Overall, a good game. This has some of my favorite mechanics in a kingdom building game, and does all of them really really well. You can get lost in this. Yet, there is a core mechanic that pushed me out: this is a pvp game based off of divides of territory each alliance has on their server. This makes the game in many cases very hard on new alliances in already-established servers. And on new servers, you have a very small window of time available to grind before you are just as screwed.

review: This is by far my best Strategy online game ever on Android. But there are a lot to be done still. 1- Background music should vary not the same over and over again. 2- A slider for zooming out and in for quick access. 3- The Commander Skill tree be a little bit more organized, for a new comer it looks confusing. 4- (Something new) Allow for alliances to become allies by having a formal tab and a communication tab. A whole new mechanic for alliances to become allies or enemies where everyone will see it. Also a diplomacy system. 5- Allow for scouts if paid extra gems to auto scout for certain amount of time. 6- Allow armies to move around more freely like in the Expedition mode. And allow them to move outside the city without having to attack or gather first then camp. It's just to long and unnecessary (in my opinion) 7- Don't screw this game up with bad micro transactions, the system that you guys have now is good. Just don't get too greedy like Kings of Avalon or etc... 8- MORE CIVILIZATIONS. Great job guys, as a PC Civilization veteran, this game is the closest I found to it in the mechanics, graphics, and music.

review: The UI UX are all quite cluttered as we can all see from mamy japanese apps. I dont understand. Do japanese prefer reading a lot of unnecessary information? Too much unrelated information which only tires my eyes, too many redundancies in the search results, too many words everywhere, images of products sometimes doesnt swipe, important details are hard to find and etc. Its just a whole mess. Can't believe this is the only online shopping app that can (barely) compete with Amazon in Japan.
